page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m1.8xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a 2.5 GHz Intel Xeon CPU. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs, utilizing KVM virtualization. The memory configuration is 32.0 GB, yielding a 1.0 GB per core ratio. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the instance in the average performance tier for most workloads, with a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1820.09 and a multi-core score of 30911.58. It demonstrates strong multi-threaded decompression capabilities, achieving 161.14 GB/sec in bzip3 tests, which falls into the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is measured at 154.63 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 69.38 GB/sec for write operations. For lightweight language model tasks, the server processes 304.01 tokens per second for text generation using a 135M parameter model, representing a strong performance tier.

The primary tradeoff of this hardware profile is its low memory-to-CPU ratio of 1:1, which limits its utility for memory-intensive databases or large-scale data caching. However, the high density of dedicated CPU cores relative to memory makes it a cost-efficient option for compute-heavy workloads that do not require large memory footprints. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded compilation, batch processing, text processing, and lightweight web serving.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m1.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-focused server featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores at 2.5 GHz and 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates average performance across most metrics, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 12692.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 34140.29. It shows strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 161.14 GB/sec and efficient text generation for small LLM models at 304.01 tokens per second. The low memory-to-core ratio represents a tradeoff, making it less suitable for memory-heavy databases but highly cost-efficient for compute-dense tasks. It is best utilized for software compilation, multi-threaded batch processing, and lightweight web serving.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m1.8xlarge is a KVM-based server with 32 Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB RAM, and 10 Gbps network bandwidth, offering strong multi-threaded decompression and compute-dense performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m1.8xlarge features 32 Intel Xeon cores and 32 GB RAM, delivering compute-dense performance for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A compute-dense, x86_64 server featuring a 1:1 CPU-to-memory ratio for efficient multi-threaded batch processing and software compilation.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performance at 161.14 GB/sec
Suited for software compilation, batch processing, and text processing
